---
title: Example session
subtitle: Introduction
session_id: session-01
event_id: null
format: revealjs
---

# Welcome

This example deck demonstrates reusable slide conventions for a teaching repository.

Use one slide deck per session.

## {data-menu-title="Learning objectives" data-state="hide-menubar"}

<br><br><br><br><br>

::: {.learning-objectives}
- explain the repository structure;
- distinguish metadata, generated data, and teaching content;
- identify which files to change when creating a new course.
:::

# Format examples {data-stack-name="Format examples"}

## Teaching goals {data-state="hide-menubar"}

The teaching goal is to make repository conventions visible before students or instructors start adding course-specific material.

::: {.highlight_must_learn}
**Must learn.**
Course repositories stay maintainable when repeated structural information lives in one metadata source.
:::

## Core concept  {data-state="hide-menubar"}

::: {.highlight_must_learn}
**Essential concept.**
A teaching repository separates reusable infrastructure, configurable metadata, generated data, and course-specific content.
:::

## Working with columns  {data-state="hide-menubar"}

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Instructor focus**

- State the learning goal.
- Connect to prior knowledge.
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
**Student focus**

- Identify assumptions.
- Ask clarification questions.
:::
::::

# Discussion prompt

::: {.discussion}
**Discuss.**
Which information belongs in `course.yml`, and which information belongs in a session file?
:::

# In-class exercise

::: {.exercise}
**Exercise.**
Open `course.yml` and identify the fields that must change for a new course.
:::

# Optional learning note

::: {.optional}
**Optional.**
Quarto projects support shared metadata, which keeps repeated document options in one place [@QuartoProjects].
:::

# Summary

::: {.highlight_must_learn}
**Take away.**
Use `course.yml` for course metadata, one source file per session artifact, generated YAML for schedule data, and Quarto for publication.
:::

Before the next session, replace the example content with course-specific material and keep reusable infrastructure generic.

# References

::: {#refs}
:::
